actions.pillar_title: Enabling world class R&D
actions.title: Establish the national HDRS
actions.action_text: Establish the national HDRS.
actions.metrics_text:
By Autumn 2025, the CEO and Chair for HDRS will be appointed.
By 2026, go live with minimum viable product for HDRS.
From December 2026, new data will be made available in HDRS on an iterative basis.
By 2027 the service will measure user satisfaction with the service’s capacity and capabilities through regular surveys, delivering continuously improving level of user feedback.
By 2030, the HDRS will provide timely access and a single point of entry to the UK’s breadth of general practice, hospital episodes, prescribing/dispensing and death registration data covering the whole population, with AI-ready datasets including linked pathology, radiology and genomic data.
Service design will be in collaboration with partners in the devolved governments.
HDRS will increase the number of data-enabled research projects, evidenced through an increase in the proportion of data access compared to data sharing and the number of data-enabled clinical trials conducted.

actions.pillar_title: Enabling world class R&D
actions.title: Government will use a combination of policy and legislative change to speed up access to health data for research and other secondary purposes, streamlining governance processes to maintain core safeguards while operating in a more efficient way
actions.action_text: Government will use a combination of policy and legislative change to speed up access to health data for research and other secondary purposes, streamlining governance processes to maintain core safeguards while operating in a more efficient way.
actions.metrics_text:
By the end of 2026, parliament permitting, pass regulations reforming the current Health Service (Control of Patient Information, COPI) Regulations of 2002 to provide a clearer, more flexible rules-based legal basis for health and care organisations (and their data processors), to access, use and share de-identified information for secondary purposes.
Subject to sufficient progress on legislative change, by the end of 2026, reform the wider NHS research data access approvals and governance system to better reflect risk and safeguards in the context of privacy protecting Secure Data Environments and move towards consolidating multiple research applications across different organisations into more streamlined processes, supporting delivery of the HDRS.
By the end of 2027, build on public engagement and work with the system, including clinical staff, to move towards national and regional models of decision making for access to all NHS data for secondary uses. GPs, other health professionals and the public will continue to have a critical role in decision making for research uses in any future system. Working collaboratively with the devolved governments and their respective approaches to accessing health data, seek to establish a common framework for UK-wide data access.

milestones.title: Implementation of reform to the wider NHS research data access approvals and governance system in relation to data access, use and sharing, and, subject to legislative progress.
milestones.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end 2026: Implementation of reform to the wider NHS research data access approvals and governance system in relation to data access, use and sharing, and, subject to legislative progress.

milestones.title: In relation to data access, use and sharing, and Parliament permitting, the government will pass regulations reforming the current Health Service (Control of Patient Information, COPI) Regulations of 2002.
milestones.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end 2026: In relation to data access, use and sharing, and Parliament permitting, the government will pass regulations reforming the current Health Service (Control of Patient Information, COPI) Regulations of 2002.

milestones.title: parliament permitting, pass regulations reforming the current Health Service (Control of Patient Information, COPI) Regulations of 2002 to provide a clearer, more flexible rules-based legal basis for health and care organisations (and their data processors), to access, use and share de-identified information for secondary purposes.
milestones.text: By the end of 2026, parliament permitting, pass regulations reforming the current Health Service (Control of Patient Information, COPI) Regulations of 2002 to provide a clearer, more flexible rules-based legal basis for health and care organisations (and their data processors), to access, use and share de-identified information for secondary purposes.

milestones.title: build on public engagement and work with the system, including clinical staff, to move towards national and regional models of decision making for access to all NHS data for secondary uses. GPs, other health professionals and the public will continue to have a critical role in decision making for research uses in any future system. Working collaboratively with the devolved governments and their respective approaches to accessing health data, seek to establish a common framework for UK-wide data access.
milestones.text: By the end of 2027, build on public engagement and work with the system, including clinical staff, to move towards national and regional models of decision making for access to all NHS data for secondary uses. GPs, other health professionals and the public will continue to have a critical role in decision making for research uses in any future system. Working collaboratively with the devolved governments and their respective approaches to accessing health data, seek to establish a common framework for UK-wide data access.

actions.pillar_title: Enabling world class R&D
actions.title: Expand UK Biobank research capability
actions.action_text: Expand and enhance the UK’s consented health research datasets and develop the cutting- edge infrastructure needed to deliver a comprehensive genomics ecosystem, maximising patient benefit, with the potential for genomics to contribute to half of all healthcare interventions by 2035 – UK BioBank
actions.metrics_text: By 2030, UK Biobank will have expanded the impact of its pre-eminent biomedical research resource with enhanced characterisation of participants. This will include proteomic analyses of all 500,000 participants alongside other multiomic data, and their health outcomes via linked primary and secondary care health records available within the UK Biobank Research Analysis Platform, maximising the value of this data.

milestones.title: UK Biobank will expand its biomedical research resource with enhanced participant characterisation.
milestones.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By 2030: UK Biobank will expand its biomedical research resource with enhanced participant characterisation.

milestones.title: UK Biobank will have expanded the impact of its pre-eminent biomedical research resource with enhanced characterisation of participants. This will include proteomic analyses of all 500,000 participants alongside other multiomic data, and their health outcomes via linked primary and secondary care health records available within the UK Biobank Research Analysis Platform, maximising the value of this data.
milestones.text: By 2030, UK Biobank will have expanded the impact of its pre-eminent biomedical research resource with enhanced characterisation of participants. This will include proteomic analyses of all 500,000 participants alongside other multiomic data, and their health outcomes via linked primary and secondary care health records available within the UK Biobank Research Analysis Platform, maximising the value of this data.

actions.pillar_title: Enabling world class R&D
actions.title: Scale Our Future Health as a clinical trials resource
actions.action_text: Expand and enhance the UK’s consented health research datasets and develop the cutting- edge infrastructure needed to deliver a comprehensive genomics ecosystem, maximising patient benefit, with the potential for genomics to contribute to half of all healthcare interventions by 2035 Our Future Health
actions.metrics_text: By 2030, Our Future Health will have become the largest longitudinal health research cohort and clinical trials resource in the world, with up to 5 million consented participants with linked primary and secondary care health data, genomic data and biobanked samples, and 10,000 incident cases for more than 100 diseases occurring in the following 2.5 years. Our Future Health will also facilitate 50,000 participants in commercial clinical trials per year by 2030 (1% of consented participants).

milestones.title: Our Future Health will have become the largest longitudinal health research cohort and clinical trials resource in the world, with up to 5 million consented participants with linked primary and secondary care health data, genomic data and biobanked samples, and 10,000 incident cases for more than 100 diseases occurring in the following 2.5 years. Our Future Health will also facilitate 50,000 participants in commercial clinical trials per year by 2030 (1% of consented participants).
milestones.text: By 2030, Our Future Health will have become the largest longitudinal health research cohort and clinical trials resource in the world, with up to 5 million consented participants with linked primary and secondary care health data, genomic data and biobanked samples, and 10,000 incident cases for more than 100 diseases occurring in the following 2.5 years. Our Future Health will also facilitate 50,000 participants in commercial clinical trials per year by 2030 (1% of consented participants).

actions.pillar_title: Enabling world class R&D
actions.title: Expand Genomics England's research database
actions.action_text: Expand and enhance the UK’s consented health research datasets and develop the cutting- edge infrastructure needed to deliver a comprehensive genomics ecosystem, maximising patient benefit, with the potential for genomics to contribute to half of all healthcare interventions by 2035 – Genomics England.
actions.metrics_text: By 2030, Genomics England will host one of the largest genomic research databases globally, with over 500,000 genomes, including those from the NHS Whole Genome Sequencing service, the Generation Study, and the Adult Population Genomics Programme. These programmes will return results to participants, while building evidence and digital infrastructure to position the UK as the leader in genomics-enabled prevention, diagnostics and clinical trials and the testing and use of genomic innovations and AI.

milestones.title: Genomics England will host one of the largest genomic research databases globally, with over 500,000 genomes, including those from the NHS Whole Genome Sequencing service, the Generation Study, and the Adult Population Genomics Programme. These programmes will return results to participants, while building evidence and digital infrastructure to position the UK as the leader in genomics-enabled prevention, diagnostics and clinical trials and the testing and use of genomic innovations and AI.
milestones.text: By 2030, Genomics England will host one of the largest genomic research databases globally, with over 500,000 genomes, including those from the NHS Whole Genome Sequencing service, the Generation Study, and the Adult Population Genomics Programme. These programmes will return results to participants, while building evidence and digital infrastructure to position the UK as the leader in genomics-enabled prevention, diagnostics and clinical trials and the testing and use of genomic innovations and AI.

milestones.title: Genomics England will host one of the largest genomic research databases globally, with over 500,000 genomes.

actions.pillar_title: Enabling world class R&D
actions.title: Use NHS genomic networks to accelerate adoption of genomic innovation
actions.action_text: Expand and enhance the UK’s consented health research datasets and develop the cutting- edge infrastructure needed to deliver a comprehensive genomics ecosystem, maximising patient benefit, with the potential for genomics to contribute to half of all healthcare interventions by 2035 – NHS Genomics Medicine Science.
actions.metrics_text: The NHS Genomics Medicine Science will work with industry and academia through the NHS Genomic Networks of Excellence to assess the latest genomic innovations in specific priority areas, such as pharmacogenomics. This will ensure that the most effective tests are accessible to patients across England via the National Genomic Test Directory. Additionally, the GMS will work with industry to streamline testing processes, reducing turnaround times so that patients receive timely results.
undated_metrics: The NHS Genomics Medicine Science will work with industry and academia through the NHS Genomic Networks of Excellence to assess the latest genomic innovations in specific priority areas, such as pharmacogenomics. This will ensure that the most effective tests are accessible to patients across England via the National Genomic Test Directory. Additionally, the GMS will work with industry to streamline testing processes, reducing turnaround times so that patients receive timely results.

actions.action_text: The government will shift investment in health R&D with a focus on primary and secondary prevention and Multiple Long-Term Conditions (MLTCs).
actions.lead_body: Department of Health and Social Care
actions.metrics_text: Over the next 5 years, we will see a sustained increase in government health and biomedical R&D funding focused on this area, utilising UK Health Research Analysis to monitor this. There is potential to supplement this with more frequent reporting and monitoring of health research funding to create a regular view of expenditure.

Number of Life Sciences companies listed on FTSE 350.
Number of IPOs by UK Life Sciences companies on the LSE and on overseas markets and amount raised overall.
Number of people employed by UK Life Sciences SMEs.

Number of IPOs by UK Life Sciences companies on the LSE and on overseas markets and amount raised overall.

actions.pillar_title: Making the UK an outstanding place to start, grow, scale, and invest
actions.title: Strengthen the Health Innovation Network to drive innovation and investment
actions.action_text: Empower the Health Innovation Network to drive innovation and investment at scale by strengthening support.
actions.metrics_text:
At least £400 million of investment leveraged through Health Innovation Network support per year.
At least 1,000 jobs safeguarded or created through Health Innovation Network support per year.
At least one million patients benefitting a year from Health Innovation Network national programmes.
undated_metrics: At least £400 million of investment leveraged through Health Innovation Network support per year.
At least 1,000 jobs safeguarded or created through Health Innovation Network support per year.
At least one million patients benefitting a year from Health Innovation Network national programmes.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Reduce barriers to market entry through faster regulation
actions.action_text: Reduce unwarranted barriers to market entry, through faster, risk-proportionate and predictable routes to regulatory approval.
actions.metrics_text:
Delivery against the MHRA’s statutory performance standards for the licensure of new medicines.
Completion of the current medical devices regulatory reform programme, including:
Publication of policy intent for early access and innovation by July 2025.
Introduction of the pre-market statutory instrument, including International Reliance Framework, to Parliament by Autumn 2026.
MHRA digital transformation for processing enquiries and applications, using AI where appropriate by 2026.

milestones.title: MHRA Publication of Medical Devices Statement of Policy Intent on Early Access and Innovation.
milestones.text: (Medtech) By July 2025: MHRA Publication of Medical Devices Statement of Policy Intent on Early Access and Innovation.

milestones.title: MHRA will be providing easily accessible scientific advice and will publish specific, published performance metrics.
milestones.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By September 2025: MHRA will be providing easily accessible scientific advice and will publish specific, published performance metrics.

milestones.title: MHRA will publish a new framework for Medical Devices, including AI.
milestones.text: (MedTech) By Summer 2026: MHRA will publish a new framework for Medical Devices, including AI.

milestones.title: For Medical Devices, DHSC will introduce a pre-market statutory instrument, including an International Reliance Framework, to Parliament.
milestones.text: (MedTech) By Autumn 2026: For Medical Devices, DHSC will introduce a pre-market statutory instrument, including an International Reliance Framework, to Parliament.

undated_metrics: Delivery against the MHRA’s statutory performance standards for the licensure of new medicines.
Completion of the current medical devices regulatory reform programme, including:
Publication of policy intent for early access and innovation by July 2025.
Introduction of the pre-market statutory instrument, including International Reliance Framework, to Parliament by Autumn 2026.
MHRA digital transformation for processing enquiries and applications, using AI where appropriate by 2026.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Ensure NICE processes are timely, agile, and transparent
actions.action_text: Streamline market entry and ensure patients receive the most effective care, by ensuring NICE processes are timely, agile and transparent
actions.metrics_text:
60% of Technology Appraisals started in 2025/2026 to be published within 240 working days of NICE’s Invitation to Participate.
EFPIA WAIT statistics on medicines access.

milestones.title: NICE will ensure 60% of Technology Appraisals started in 2025/2026 are published within 240 working days of NICE’s Invitation to Participate.
milestones.text: (Pharma) By end 2026: NICE will ensure 60% of Technology Appraisals started in 2025/2026 are published within 240 working days of NICE’s Invitation to Participate.

undated_metrics: 60% of Technology Appraisals started in 2025/2026 to be published within 240 working days of NICE’s Invitation to Participate.
EFPIA WAIT statistics on medicines access.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Enhance MHRA and NICE coordination for faster market entry
actions.action_text: Streamline market entry, though enhanced coordination between the MHRA and NICE.
actions.metrics_text:
By April 2026, provide easily accessible provision of high-quality integrated scientific advice, with specific, published performance metrics on timelines to respond to requests for scientific advice and timelines to meetings occurring.
The Innovative Device Access Pathway (IDAP) to be rolled out as a permanent programme by April 2027, and the refresh of the Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway (ILAP) will continue. Both pathways will have published KPIs on the number of companies and variety of products supported

milestones.title: provide easily accessible provision of high-quality integrated scientific advice, with specific, published performance metrics on timelines to respond to requests for scientific advice and timelines to meetings occurring.
milestones.text: By April 2026, provide easily accessible provision of high-quality integrated scientific advice, with specific, published performance metrics on timelines to respond to requests for scientific advice and timelines to meetings occurring.

milestones.title: The innovative Device Access Pathway (IDAP) will be rolled out as a permanent programme and complete the refresh of the Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway (ILAP).
milestones.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By April 2027: The innovative Device Access Pathway (IDAP) will be rolled out as a permanent programme and complete the refresh of the Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway (ILAP).

undated_metrics: The Innovative Device Access Pathway (IDAP) to be rolled out as a permanent programme by April 2027, and the refresh of the Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway (ILAP) will continue. Both pathways will have published KPIs on the number of companies and variety of products supported

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Reduce friction so effective medicines reach patients faster
actions.action_text: Reduce friction in the system to optimise access and uptake of new medicines so the most clinically and cost-effective can reach patients faster.
actions.metrics_text:
Medicines uptake reported within the Life Sciences Competitiveness Indicators and Innovation Scorecard.
IQVIA reporting on biosimilars uptake.
undated_metrics: Medicines uptake reported within the Life Sciences Competitiveness Indicators and Innovation Scorecard.
IQVIA reporting on biosimilars uptake.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Create low-friction procurement and contracting for MedTech
actions.action_text: Streamline access and adoption of MedTech by reducing duplication and introducing low- friction procurement and contracting mechanisms.
actions.lead_body: Department of Health and Social Care
actions.metrics_text:
The number of MedTech HTAs.
The number of products receiving reimbursement through the RBP and Value Based Procurement mechanisms, and their consequent level of uptake.

milestones.title: Value Based Procurement Pilot Launch.
milestones.text:
(Medtech) By end 2025: Value Based
Procurement Pilot Launch.

milestones.title: RBP will be launched, including referrals to the NICE Technology Appraisal process.
milestones.text: (MedTech) From April 2026: RBP will be launched, including referrals to the NICE Technology Appraisal process.

milestones.title: The NHS will introduce an Innovator Passport.
milestones.text: (MedTech) By end 2026: The NHS will introduce an Innovator Passport.

undated_metrics: The number of MedTech HTAs.
The number of products receiving reimbursement through the RBP and Value Based Procurement mechanisms, and their consequent level of uptake.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Place a growth mandate on NHS commercial activity
actions.action_text: The government will place a Growth Mandate on NHS commercial activity including NHS Supply Chain and within the Medicines Procurement and Supply Chain Frameworks.
actions.metrics_text: The proportion of procurements completed using the Value Based Procurement methodology, and their consequent level of uptake, measured across companies of all sizes to enable comparison between large organisations and SMEs.,br> - The proportion of tenders using the Value Based Procurement methodology, and their consequent level of uptake, measured across companies.
undated_metrics: The proportion of procurements completed using the Value Based Procurement methodology, and their consequent level of uptake, measured across companies of all sizes to enable comparison between large organisations and SMEs.,br> - The proportion of tenders using the Value Based Procurement methodology, and their consequent level of uptake, measured across companies.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Update and expand the Innovation Scorecard
actions.action_text: Strengthen innovation metrics for medicines and MedTech through an updated and expanded Innovation Scorecard.
actions.metrics_text:
Number of products/groupings for which an estimate of expected uptake is published in the Innovation Scorecard.
Number of MedTech products with published uptake data.
undated_metrics: Number of products/groupings for which an estimate of expected uptake is published in the Innovation Scorecard.
Number of MedTech products with published uptake data.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Deliver the Healthcare Goals programme
actions.action_text: Deliver the ambitions of the government’s Healthcare Goals programme, across Addiction, Cancer, Dementia, Mental Health, and Obesity, with continued significant government funding.
actions.metrics_text: Each Goals programme is focused on the delivery of quantifiable and timebound KPIs. These will be published on the Healthcare Goals webpage following the outcome of Phase 2 of the Spending Review 2025.

milestones.title: Realised the ambitions of the Healthcare Goals by meeting the programme’s Key Performance Indicators.
milestones.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By the end of 2030: Realised the ambitions of the Healthcare Goals by meeting the programme’s Key Performance Indicators.

undated_metrics: Each Goals programme is focused on the delivery of quantifiable and timebound KPIs. These will be published on the Healthcare Goals webpage following the outcome of Phase 2 of the Spending Review 2025.

actions.pillar_title: Driving health innovation and NHS reform
actions.title: Establish Regional Health Innovation Zones
actions.action_text: Establish Regional Health Innovation Zones for large scale development and implementation of innovation, for scale-up across the health and care system.
actions.metrics_text:
Number of alternative delivery pathways trialled and ready to be scaled nationally.
Number of patients benefitting from new care delivery pathways
Investment leveraged through industry co-partnership.
undated_metrics: Number of alternative delivery pathways trialled and ready to be scaled nationally.
Number of patients benefitting from new care delivery pathways
Investment leveraged through industry co-partnership.

roadmap.text: (Medtech) By July 2025: MHRA Publication of Medical Devices Statement of Policy Intent on Early Access and Innovation.

roadmap.title: MHRA will be providing easily accessible scientific advice and will publish specific, published performance metrics.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By September 2025: MHRA will be providing easily accessible scientific advice and will publish specific, published performance metrics.

roadmap.title: The Global Talent Taskforce will be established, which will help attract talent from the IS-8, including Life Sciences.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) Summer 2025: The Global Talent Taskforce will be established, which will help attract talent from the IS-8, including Life Sciences.

roadmap.title: Turing AI Pioneer Fellowship applications open which Life Sciences Talent can apply to.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) Autumn 2025: Turing AI Pioneer Fellowship applications open which Life Sciences Talent can apply to.

roadmap.title: government will publish a 10 Year UK Research Workforce Strategy and a 3 year implementation plan for England.
roadmap.text:
(Pharma and MedTech) By Autumn 2025: government will publish a 10 Year UK Research
Workforce Strategy and a 3 year implementation plan for England.

roadmap.title: Publication of NIHR’s improving health and economic growth delivery plan.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By October 2025: Publication of NIHR’s improving health and economic growth delivery plan.

roadmap.title: Value Based Procurement Pilot Launch.
roadmap.text:
(Medtech) By end 2025: Value Based
Procurement Pilot Launch.

roadmap.title: The OLS will work with the Futures Group on a series of skills-focused industry workshops.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end 2025: The OLS will work with the Futures Group on a series of skills-focused industry workshops.

roadmap.title: The Department for Education will develop a new Post-16 Education and Skills Strategy, fulfilling a key government manifesto commitment.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By the end of 2025: The Department for Education will develop a new Post-16 Education and Skills Strategy, fulfilling a key government manifesto commitment.

roadmap.title: Commercial Clinical Trial approval and set up time will be 150 days or less.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By March 2026: Commercial Clinical Trial approval and set up time will be 150 days or less.

roadmap.title: UKRI will have delivered improved coordination with NIHR activity and be working to support SMEs.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By March 2026: UKRI will have delivered improved coordination with NIHR activity and be working to support SMEs.

roadmap.title: UKRI will have identified game-changing technologies for IUK Biomedical Catalyst support.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By March 2026: UKRI will have identified game-changing technologies for IUK Biomedical Catalyst support.

roadmap.title: RBP will be launched, including referrals to the NICE Technology Appraisal process.
roadmap.text: (MedTech) From April 2026: RBP will be launched, including referrals to the NICE Technology Appraisal process.

roadmap.title: The BBB will begin deploying new Industrial Strategy capital.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end Q1 2026/2027: The BBB will begin deploying new Industrial Strategy capital.

roadmap.title: The BBB will enhance monitoring and reporting functions to track investments into IS sectors.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end Q1 2026/2027: The BBB will enhance monitoring and reporting functions to track investments into IS sectors.

roadmap.title: DHSC will have created a single searchable database of clinical trial activity.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By June 2026: DHSC will have created a single searchable database of clinical trial activity.

roadmap.title: Complete implementation of 7 healthcare Centres of Excellence in Regulatory Science and Innovation.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) Spring 2026: Complete implementation of 7 healthcare Centres of Excellence in Regulatory Science and Innovation.

roadmap.title: Launch of the minimum viable product for the HDRS.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By September 2026: Launch of the minimum viable product for the HDRS.

roadmap.title: MHRA will publish a new framework for Medical Devices, including AI.
roadmap.text: (MedTech) By Summer 2026: MHRA will publish a new framework for Medical Devices, including AI.

roadmap.title: For Medical Devices, DHSC will introduce a pre-market statutory instrument, including an International Reliance Framework, to Parliament.
roadmap.text: (MedTech) By Autumn 2026: For Medical Devices, DHSC will introduce a pre-market statutory instrument, including an International Reliance Framework, to Parliament.

roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) From December 2026: New data assets will be brought into scope for the HDRS on a prioritised basis.

roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end 2026: NIHR will launch an AI Research Screening Platform.

roadmap.text:
(- Pharma and MedTech) By end 2026: As part of our pre-clinical translational infrastructure, MRC will
Establish a Pre-Clinical Translational Hub.

roadmap.title: Implementation of reform to the wider NHS research data access approvals and governance system in relation to data access, use and sharing, and, subject to legislative progress.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end 2026: Implementation of reform to the wider NHS research data access approvals and governance system in relation to data access, use and sharing, and, subject to legislative progress.

roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By end 2026: Launch the NIHR Innovation Catalyst.

roadmap.text: (MedTech) By end 2026: The NHS will introduce an Innovator Passport.

roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By April 2027: The innovative Device Access Pathway (IDAP) will be rolled out as a permanent programme and complete the refresh of the Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway (ILAP).

roadmap.title: HDRS will begin creating UK-wide service.
roadmap.text:

HDRS will begin creating UK-wide service.

roadmap.title: Industry engagement will launchas part of a review of priority skills needs of the Life Sciences sector.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By the end of 2027: Industry engagement will launchas part of a review of priority skills needs of the Life Sciences sector.

roadmap.title: MRC will establish a further 2 translational research networks.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By 2028: MRC will establish a further 2 translational research networks.

roadmap.title: Genomics England will host one of the largest genomic research databases globally, with over 500,000 genomes.

roadmap.title: Realised the ambitions of the Healthcare Goals by meeting the programme’s Key Performance Indicators.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By the end of 2030: Realised the ambitions of the Healthcare Goals by meeting the programme’s Key Performance Indicators.

roadmap.title: There will have been a sustained increase in R&D funding towards prevention and Multiple Long-Term Conditions – tracked via ‘UK Health Research Analysis’ data.
roadmap.text: (Pharma and MedTech) By the end of 2030: There will have been a sustained increase in R&D funding towards prevention and Multiple Long-Term Conditions – tracked via ‘UK Health Research Analysis’ data.

roadmap.title: The OLS, Department of Education and Department for Business and Trade to review activity across all skills actions and set priorities.
